pageName: What is TiDB? Distributed SQL Database

meta.title: What is TiDB? Distributed SQL Database
meta.description: TiDB is a distributed SQL database that combines the best of traditional databases with horizontal scalability. Learn how TiDB powers modern applications.

props.headline: The Distributed SQL Database Built for Scale
props.subheadline: TiDB combines the familiarity of traditional SQL with the scalability of NoSQL. Get started with a database that grows with your business.

props.title: Why Choose TiDB?
props.subtitle: TiDB delivers enterprise-grade reliability with cloud-native scalability

items.title: Horizontal Scalability
items.description: Scale your database seamlessly across multiple nodes without resharding data manually.

items.title: ACID Transactions
items.description: Maintain data integrity with full ACID compliance across distributed transactions.

items.title: SQL Compatible
items.description: Use standard SQL syntax. TiDB is compatible with MySQL, making migration effortless.

items.title: Cloud-Native
items.description: Run on Kubernetes or any cloud infrastructure. Deploy where you need, when you need.

props.title: How TiDB Works
props.subtitle: A modern distributed architecture designed for performance and reliability

items.title: TiDB Server
items.description: Stateless SQL layer that processes queries and coordinates transactions across the cluster.

items.title: TiKV Storage
items.description: Distributed key-value store with strong consistency and high availability guarantees.

items.title: TiFlash
items.description: Columnar storage engine for fast analytical queries on the same data.

items.title: PD Placement Driver
items.description: Manages cluster topology, balances data distribution, and handles failover automatically.

items.title: E-Commerce Platforms

items.title: Analytics & BI

items.title: IoT & Time Series

items.title: Financial Services

items.label: Licensing
items.description: Open source with commercial support options

items.q: How is TiDB different from traditional databases?
items.a: TiDB is a distributed SQL database that automatically scales horizontally while maintaining ACID transactions. Traditional databases require manual sharding and typically sacrifice consistency for scale. TiDB gives you both.
Yes. TiDB uses MySQL protocol and syntax, making it a drop-in replacement for many MySQL applications. Most existing tools and drivers work without modification.

items.q: Can I run TiDB in the cloud?
items.a: TiDB Cloud is our fully managed service available on AWS, Google Cloud, and Azure. You can also self-host TiDB on any Kubernetes cluster or cloud infrastructure.

items.q: What kind of performance should I expect?
items.a: Performance depends on your workload, hardware, and configuration. TiDB typically delivers microsecond latency for OLTP and near-real-time results for analytical queries using TiFlash.

items.q: How does TiDB handle high availability?
items.a: TiDB uses Raft consensus for data replication. Data is replicated across multiple nodes, and the system automatically handles node failures with zero data loss.

props.title: Ready to Experience TiDB?
props.subtitle: Join thousands of companies using TiDB for their most demanding workloads.
